description_long: This road roller features a compact, rounded yellow body with a sloped roof and three large, textured cylindrical drums for rolling surfaces. The upper section has a set of black ventilation slats and a small antenna, suggesting some level of remote or automated control. The front face is equipped with two hexagonal headlights and a black grille, while the rear has two circular red lights. The wheels are robust and deeply treaded, designed for effective traction on urban construction sites. The surface appears to be made of painted metal, with some visible wear and subtle texturing. There is no enclosed operator cabin, only a small control panel on the roof, indicating it may be remotely operated or autonomous.
description: A compact yellow road roller with three large, textured drums, a sloped roof with vents and antenna, and no enclosed cabin. It features prominent headlights, a control panel, and rugged construction for urban use.
description_view_0: Side view showing the sloped roof with black vents, the antenna, and three large, textured roller drums.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the hexagonal headlights, black grille, and the control panel with green indicators on the roof.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, with the exhaust pipe visible on the left side and the three roller drums.
description_view_3: Rear view showing two circular red lights and the rounded back of the body, with the roller drums and axle details visible.
